_comment: Single source of truth consumed by .github/workflows/coverage.yml AND tools/scripts/local_diff_cover.sh. To change the threshold, surface set, or per-file exclusions, edit here once. `diff_cover_excludes` is the line-coverage-exclude list passed to `diff-cover --exclude=...` — use sparingly for thin dispatchers / generated code that is exercised end-to-end via shell-out tests but doesn't propagate llvm-cov instrumentation. PATTERN CONTRACT: each entry is matched via fnmatch against (a) the file's basename and (b) its absolute path. Literal relative paths like `tools/cli/cmd_loop.cpp` match NEITHER and are silent no-ops. Use a basename (`cmd_loop.cpp`) or a glob (`**/cmd_loop.cpp`). The script + workflow pass all entries under a SINGLE `--exclude val1 val2 ...` flag because diff-cover's argparse `nargs='+'` overwrites on repeat. cmd_loop.cpp's watch-loop entry path (lines ~280-306) cannot be exercised by a shellout test (the loop blocks until SIGINT), so the file is exercised end-to-end via the --no-watch shellout tests in test/test_cli_shellout.cpp instead. scanner_clap.cpp + cmd_projects.cpp were added during the Phase 8 integration PR (#1005): scanner_clap.cpp has LCOV_EXCL_START..STOP regions wrapping defensive try/catch on the dlopen boundary (#812 hardening) but the diff-cover tool doesn't honor LCOV markers (markers are gcov-specific; llvm-cov-export -> diff-cover loses them). cmd_projects.cpp's new --json path is exercised end-to-end via test/test_cli_shellout.cpp shellout tests but not by direct unit-test instrumentation. pulp_import_design.cpp, cg_canvas.mm, and window_host_mac.mm are current main exclusions for harness/import-design and macOS renderer paths. File a follow-up to thread `lcov --remove` or equivalent into the coverage pipeline so LCOV_EXCL markers propagate to diff-cover, then drop temporary entries from this list when native coverage catches up. cmd_coverage.cpp was previously excluded for the same reason but is now properly counted: local_diff_cover.sh now mirrors run_coverage.sh's wider object-discovery pattern (build/tools, build/inspect, build/bindings) so CLI shell-out tests propagate llvm-cov instrumentation through pulp-cli / pulp-standalone / pulp-inspect (#919 follow-up).
